name: Jocasta Innes Dur Mou
recipeInstructions:
This old French family recipe starts with a basic chocolate mousse mixture made
with lots of eggs, and ends up with something between a soufflé and a squidgy
cake. You then sandwich a layer of the chocolate mousse between two layers of the
dur mou for an unbelievable, wickedly rich result.
1. Preheat the oven to 300°F.
2. In a bowl placed over a pan of simmering water, melt the chocolate with the butter.
3. Beat the sugar with the egg yolks until smooth.
4. Mix this with the melted chocolate away from the heat, adding it gradually.
5. Beat the egg whites in a clean bowl with a tiny pinch of salt until stiff.
6. Fold the egg white mixture into the chocolate mixture gently but thoroughly.
7. Have ready two greased 7-inch cake tins.
8. Divide about two thirds of the cake mixture between the pans and bake in the oven for 1 hour.
9. Meanwhile, keep the remaining cake mixture/mousse in the fridge.
10. Let the cakes cool.
11. Spoon the mousse over the cake base and sandwich the two halves together.
12. Eat warm or cold.
